headline: Clear scans, real pain
body: In pain-free adults, MRI 'abnormalities' are extremely common, and rise with age.
caption:
Here is a finding that reframes a lot of worry. Much of what scans label as damage is also present in people with no pain at all.

A large review of imaging in pain-free adults found disc bulges in more than half of people in their thirties, rising with age. Disc degeneration, protrusions and other 'abnormalities' were common in people who felt completely fine. The picture on the scan often does not match the pain in the person.

This matters because, in chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID, fear of damage can keep the nervous system's alarm running. Understanding that a scan is not a sentence is part of how recovery begins.

Learn more with a practitioner at https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: The study: Brinjikji W et al. (2015), Systematic literature review of imaging features of spinal degeneration in asymptomatic populations, American Journal of Neuroradiology, 36(4), 811 to 816. Disc bulges and degeneration were common in pain-free people and increased with age. https://www.ajnr.org/content/36/4/811
image_source: pomelli jul: man reading letter, calm relief (text inpainted)
